name: Biceps Brachii
placement: Mid-humeral level, anterior compartment. Long head and medial heads targeted separately.
setup: Surface anatomy sufficient, but USG avoids Brachialis if targeted alone.
usgInstructions: Probe transverse over mid-humerus. Identify Biceps as the superficial layer. Deep to Biceps is the Brachialis muscle, separated by the musculocutaneous nerve.

name: Brachialis
placement: Distal 1/3 of humerus, deep to Biceps Brachii.
setup: USG critical to avoid Biceps and musculocutaneous nerve.
usgInstructions: Probe transverse over distal 1/3 of humerus. Identify the musculoskeletal unit deep to the Biceps. Entry often lateral or medial to the Biceps belly.

name: Triceps Brachii
placement: Posterior compartment of humerus. Long head, lateral head, and medial head targeted.
setup: Surface anatomy or EMG usually sufficient.
usgInstructions: Probe transverse over posterior arm. Identify three distinct muscle heads. Use Doppler to avoid the Radial artery/nerve in the spiral groove.

name: Flexor Carpi Radialis
placement: Proximal 1/3 of forearm, radial side of the midline.
setup: USG recommended to avoid Radial artery.
usgInstructions: Identify target at proximal 1/3 of forearm. FCR is superficial and lateral to the FDS. Locate Radial Artery laterally.

name: Flexor Carpi Ulnaris
placement: Proximal 2/5 of forearm on ulnar border.
setup: USG critical to avoid Ulnar nerve/artery.
usgInstructions: Identify target at proximal forearm, ulnar border. FCU sits superficial to FDP. The Ulnar neurovascular bundle lies deep/lateral to FCU.

name: Gastrocnemius
landmarks: Medial/Lateral condyles of femur, Achilles tendon.
placement: Medial and Lateral heads targeted in the proximal 1/3 of the lower leg.
setup: Surface anatomy sufficient, but USG avoids Soleus if targeted separately.
usgInstructions: Probe transverse over proximal calf. Identify Medial and Lateral heads as the superficial layers.

name: Soleus
landmarks: Deep to Gastrocnemius, Medial/Lateral calf borders.
placement: Mid-distal calf, targeted deep to or below the Gastrocnemius bellies.
setup: USG critical to avoid Gastrocnemius if injecting Soleus only.
usgInstructions: Identify target deep to the Gastrocnemius. Best viewed laterally or medially where Soleus extends beyond Gastroc bellies.

name: Tibialis Anterior
placement: Proximal 1/3 of anterior leg, just lateral to the tibia.
usgInstructions: Probe transverse over proximal anterior leg. Identify Tib Ant lateral to the tibial bone shadow.

name: Rectus Femoris
placement: Anterior mid-thigh, most superficial of the quadriceps.
setup: Surface anatomy or USG. USG allows separation from Vastus Intermedius.
usgInstructions: Probe transverse over mid-thigh. Rectus Femoris is the most superficial 'bullseye' muscle.
